name: Sierra de Ayllón, Puerto del Infante y El Muyo — circular desde Ayllón
description: Challenge yourself on this 40.0-mile mountain bike route through Sierra de Ayllón, featuring technical terrain and stunning views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Ayllón traverse a diverse landscape in the province of Segovia, Spain, characterized by varied topography. The region features the rugged Sierra de Ayllón mountain range, with its rocky peaks and V-shaped valleys. Extensive forests, including notable beech forests like Hayedo de Tejera Negra, offer dense woodlands and varied tree species. River gorges, such as those carved by the Riaza and Duratón rivers, add to the challenging and scenic terrain available for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Ayllón The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Ayllón, Puerto del Infante y El Muyo — circular desde Ayllón, a difficult 39.9 miles (64.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 39 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Guadalajara - Segovia – circular desde Campisábalos por la senda de los Caracoles, a difficult 26.7 miles (43.0 km) path. This route navigates through varied landscapes, including sections near river valleys. Local mountain bikers also love the Becerril – Villacorta loop from Santa María de Riaza, a 26.6 miles (42.7 km) trail leading through rural areas and hills, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Ayllón is defined by rocky peaks, dense woodlands, and river gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Ayllón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ayllón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ayllón?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails available around Ayllón, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes through rugged mountain ranges, dense beech forests, and scenic river gorges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ayllón?
A: The terrain around Ayllón is highly varied. You can expect rocky peaks, steep V-shaped valleys, and extensive forests, including notable beech forests. Many routes also navigate through river gorges, offering a mix of challenging ascents and descents, as well as more scenic paths along river valleys.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Ayllón?
A: Yes, while many trails offer challenging terrain, there are also options suitable for less experienced riders and families. Out of the 34 available routes, 2 are classified as easy, and 26 are moderate, providing accessible choices for a range of skill levels. For example, you might find gentler paths near river valleys or in less rugged forested areas.

Q: What are some natural attractions or points of interest I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Ayllón's trails pass through a region rich in natural beauty. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to Hayedo de Tejera Negra Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its ancient beech forests. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1733842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Aguisejo River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2490089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maderuelo and the Linares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and various viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matarredonda Meadow Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also characterized by impressive river gorges like those of the Riaza and Duratón rivers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Ayllón?
A: Yes, many routes in Ayllón are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Becerril – Villacorta loop from Santa María de Riaza&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through rural areas and hills. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Aguisejo River – Park entrance loop from La Senda de los Caracoles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ayllón?
A: The diverse landscapes of Ayllón offer good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, especially in the beech forests. Summer can be warm, but higher altitude trails or routes through shaded woodlands can still be enjoyable. Winter riding is possible, but conditions can be colder, and some higher areas might experience snow or ice.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Ayllón?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's important to check specific regulations for protected areas like the Hayedo de Tejera Negra or the Natural Parks of the Hoces del Río Riaza and Duratón, as rules regarding pets may vary. Always keep your dog under control and respect local wildlife.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Ayllón?
A: The mountain bike trails around Ayllón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from around 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic routes through dense woodlands and along river gorges, as well as the rich natural landscapes and diverse flora and fauna.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in certain areas around Ayllón?
A: Some protected natural areas, such as the Hayedo de Tejera Negra Natural Park, may require permits or have specific access rules, especially during peak seasons, to preserve their delicate ecosystems. It's advisable to check with local park authorities before planning your ride, particularly if your route passes through such designated areas.

Q: Is public transport available to reach mountain bike trailheads in Ayllón?
A: Public transport options directly to specific trailheads in the more remote parts of the Sierra de Ayllón can be limited. Ayllón itself is accessible by bus from larger towns, but reaching many of the mountain biking starting points often requires personal vehicle transport. It's recommended to plan your logistics accordingly.

Q: Where can I find parking near the mountain bike trails in Ayllón?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of Ayllón, as well as in smaller villages that serve as common starting points for routes. For trails entering natural parks or specific attractions, designated parking areas are often provided, such as near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to Hayedo de Tejera Negra Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Always look for official parking to avoid obstructing local access or damaging natural areas.
tips: We are at 1,484 meters of altitude with splendid views of the area that surrounds the Collado del Puerto de los Infantes. From this point you can continue towards the Valdebecerril peak (1,766 m) following the fence that separates the provinces of Segovia and Guadalajara. You will be surprised by the magnificent views of the Hayedo de Tejera Negra Natural Park.

El Muyo is a beautiful town, district of the municipality of Riaza, in the province of Segovia. Known as a ''black'' town due to the slate constructions that give that dark and characteristic color to the village. Although not many people live there, on weekends it is more crowded by sports and nature fans and you can enjoy a good atmosphere. There is a bar at the entrance to the town with a terrace and gardens where you can have a drink. Also noteworthy is the 14th century church with many details of art and historical treasures.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a challenging mountain bike route categorized as 'difficult'. It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills, with technical sections rated S3-S6. Some parts may even require you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain typical of the Iberian mountains, known for its 'dusty and loose' conditions. Be prepared for 'fierce slate rock and super technical steep features' throughout the 64.3 km loop, which includes a substantial 960 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this challenging mountain bike loop is approximately 5 hours and 40 minutes. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, technical skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers splendid views, especially around the Collado del Puerto de los Infantes. You'll also pass through the settlement of El Muyo. The region is known for its expansive vistas of wild Iberian mountains, blue skies, and the possibility of seeing wandering cattle and vultures.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Ayllón region is considered to offer some of the best autumn riding in Europe, suggesting that fall is an excellent time to experience this route. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Ayllón. While specific parking details for the trailhead aren't provided, you would typically look for parking options within or on the outskirts of Ayllón town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dog-friendliness for this specific trail is not available. When mountain biking with dogs, it's generally advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to ensure they have the fitness for such a demanding route.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to ride this mountain bike route. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and length (64.3 km), you should bring plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves), and navigation tools. Due to technical sections, consider knee and elbow pads.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Ayllón?
faqs.answer: Ayllón is a town, so it is highly likely you will find cafes, pubs, and restaurants there to refuel before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially if you plan an early start or late finish.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several official paths, including sections of the Ruta del Sureste, Camino Natural de la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, Camino a Martín Muñoz, Calle la Campana, Camino El Monte, Camino de Arriba al Molino, Camino de Tinadas, Carretera vieja, Camino Rural, and Calle las Peñas.

waypoints.name: Collado del Puerto de los Infantes
